seo.meta_title: Tree Service in Lawton, OK | Lawton Tree Pros
seo.meta_description: Professional tree service in Lawton, OK. Licensed & insured. Free estimates. Emergency tree removal available 24/7. Call (580) 781-1781.

testimonials.name: Brian W.
testimonials.text: Had a large oak drop a limb on my fence after a storm. Lawton Tree Pros were out the next morning, removed everything, and the cleanup was spotless. Fair price too.

testimonials.name: Deb S.
testimonials.text: Used them for a full tree removal and stump grinding in my backyard. Professional crew, done in half a day. Will absolutely call again.

testimonials.text: Free estimate was fast, no pressure, and the price was very fair. Trimmed three large trees and cleaned up every branch. Highly recommend.

faqs.q: How much does tree removal cost in Lawton, OK?
faqs.a: Tree removal in Lawton typically ranges from $350 to $2,200 depending on tree size, location, and accessibility. Small trees under 30 ft average $350–$600, large oaks or cottonwoods over 60 ft run $1,000–$2,200. We provide free on-site estimates with no obligation.

faqs.q: Do you offer emergency tree service in Lawton?
faqs.a: Yes — we offer 24/7 emergency tree service throughout Lawton and Comanche County. Storm damage, fallen trees blocking driveways or damaging structures handled around the clock. Call (580) area number anytime.

faqs.q: Is tree removal covered by homeowners insurance in Oklahoma?
faqs.a: Oklahoma homeowners insurance typically covers tree removal when a fallen tree damages a covered structure (home, fence, garage). Removal of a standing hazard tree that hasn't caused damage is usually not covered. We work directly with insurance adjusters and provide full documentation.

faqs.q: Do you serve Elgin, Cache, and surrounding Comanche County communities?
faqs.a: Yes — we serve all of Comanche County including Elgin, Cache, Fletcher, Waurika, and extend service to Duncan, Chickasha, Anadarko, and Hobart. Call us for service availability in your area.

faqs.q: How long does tree removal take?
faqs.a: Most residential tree removals take 2–6 hours. Larger trees or those near power lines or structures may take a full day. We handle all cleanup and debris removal as part of every job.
